headline: What Camp Dream GA does is create room for campers to belong, participate, and be celebrated
subheadline: Camp Dream GA serves children and young adults with disabilities through overnight summer camp, year-round Camp Out gatherings, and a volunteer culture designed around relationship, support, and joy.

sections.title: Summer Camp
sections.description: The overnight camp sessions remain the centerpiece of the ministry and the clearest picture of the mission in action.

sections.title: Camp Out
sections.description: Year-round gatherings help the community remain connected and give supporters more ways to stay engaged.

sections.title: Volunteer and donor mobilization
sections.description: Camp Dream GA depends on people who give their time, skill, and financial support so campers can have a fuller experience.

faq.question: How can someone help without volunteering at camp?
faq.answer: Giving financially, sharing the mission, connecting supporters, and following year-round updates are all meaningful ways to help.

cta.heading: Volunteer if you want to serve directly, or donate if you want to help sustain the mission behind every session.
cta.body: Camp Dream GA works best when the community can quickly understand both the mission and the action paths connected to it.
